subject: Ecology
title: Urban Evolution
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Urban Evolution.

hook.text: Cities change so fast that some species evolve on a human timescale—decades, not millennia.

buildup.text: Urban lizards in Puerto Rico evolved stickier toe pads to grip smooth concrete surfaces.

discovery.text: City blackbirds in Europe have shorter wings and different stress hormones than forest ones.

twist.text: White clover in cities lost its cyanide defense—there's less herbivory but more freeze stress.

climax.text: Urban evolution is now one of the fastest-growing fields in modern ecology.

quiz.question: How have urban lizards in Puerto Rico evolved?
options: Stickier toe pads for smooth surfaces
